feed.title: HealthRankings — Health News
feed.subtitle: Latest US health headlines. Full list: https://healthrankings.co/healthrankings-news.html

entry.title: Americans All Negative For Hantavirus, French Patient Critically Ill (Live Updates)
entry.summary: The American who tested positive for hantavirus says he is symptom free while a patient in Paris is critically ill.

entry.title: AI can design viruses, toxins and other bioweapons. How worried should we be?
entry.summary: Scientists are debating whether to limit biological AI software to ward off threats.

entry.title: PCOS Is Officially Renamed, After Decades of Misinformation
entry.summary: First, it was named after two men.

entry.title: I'm A 41-Year-Old Single Mom. I'm Over Dating Men My Own Age.
entry.summary: “I didn’t always date younger men. In fact, just after my divorce at age 33, I actively avoided it.”

entry.title: You should probably eat more fiber. Here's why — and how to do it : Short Wave
entry.summary: The average person eats 10-15 grams of fiber per day, according to the USDA. The problem? That’s WAY under the recommended daily amount. Fiber – a type of carb that our bodies are unable to digest – is prevalent in foods like fruits, veggies, whole grains, an…

entry.title: A Common Vitamin Has a Complicated Link to Cancer, Experts Reveal
entry.summary: We've all heard the advice: eat your fruit and vegetables, get your vitamins, and stay healthy.

entry.title: Is hantavirus a pandemic risk? Medical experts discuss comparison to COVID-19
entry.summary: Imagery of health officials in full personal protective equipment quarantining people from a Dutch cruise ship has driven memories of the coronavirus pandemic. WCPO asked, is there a risk?

entry.title: 3 King County residents possibly exposed to hantavirus linked to cruise
entry.summary: There are currently no cases of hantavirus in King County, and risk to the general public remains low.

entry.title: Hantavirus outbreak tests Trump officials who criticized covid response
entry.summary: Some top administration health figures had publicly questioned the credibility of the agencies that are now central to explaining a new and uncertain threat.

entry.title: Scientists Debunk Seed Oil Myths in Major New Nutrition Review
entry.summary: Seed oils like canola and soybean are safe and may have health benefits, a new review shows. Experts address inflammation, heart health, and more.

entry.title: Health officials are monitoring Minnesotan potentially exposed to hantavirus
entry.summary: Hantaviruses are a group of viruses carried by rodents that can be transmitted to humans, often causing serious illness.

entry.title: UNC-Chapel Hill Coronavirus Researcher Ralph Baric to Retire
entry.summary: Baric’s life’s work paved the way for vaccines against COVID-19. But it brought scrutiny to him, his lab, and UNC in the process.

entry.title: Slowing Parkinson’s by Blocking a Key Protein
entry.summary: Researchers identify GPNMB as a driver of Parkinson’s disease spread and demonstrate that monoclonal antibodies can block its activity to slow neurodegeneration.

entry.title: 3 King County residents being monitored for hantavirus
entry.summary: Three King County residents may have been exposed to the hantavirus that has killed and sickened passengers aboard the MV Hondius cruise ship.

entry.title: Psychology says the loneliest part of being 70 isn’t being alone, it’s being in rooms full of people who love you but no longer expect you to have anything to say
entry.summary: It's the invisibility factor.

entry.title: 'Very contagious' parvovirus swirling throughout Northern California
entry.summary: Parvovirus B19 is a contagious respiratory illness that can cause a distinct rosy rash among children and lead to severe complications among pregnant people.

entry.title: 5 ways to get more vitamin D without taking a supplement
entry.summary: You can get more of the “sunshine vitamin” from food than you think.

entry.title: All Your Hantavirus Questions, Answered by an Infectious Disease Expert
entry.summary: Here’s what you need to know, from why the cruise ship outbreak won’t spark the next pandemic to how hantavirus spreads.

entry.title: Polyendocrine Metabolic Ovarian Syndrome: New name to improve diagnosis and care of condition affecting 170 million women worldwide
entry.summary: Polyendocrine Metabolic Ovarian Syndrome (PMOS) is the new name for the condition previously known as Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS), which impacts 1 in 8, or more than 170 million women worldwide.

entry.title: Scientists reversed biological age in older adults with a 4-week diet change
entry.summary: A four-week diet change was enough to make some older adults appear biologically younger in a new University of Sydney study. Participants who reduced fat intake or shifted toward more plant-based protein showed improvements in key health biomarkers tied to a…

entry.title: Could Argentina hantavirus outbreak be a problem for the World Cup?
entry.summary: Argentina is battling one of its largest outbreaks of hantavirus in years, with deaths rising.

entry.title: Scientists Just Rewrote Biology: “Hidden” Mechanism Could Transform Diabetes Treatment
entry.summary: Researchers have uncovered an unexpected biological process that may allow the body to directly control stored sugar, challenging decades of scientific understanding.

entry.title: The Science on Creatine Dosage Is Shifting – Here’s How Many Grams You Should Take
entry.summary: Some wellness influencers recommend five times the usual – we talked to researchers for the real scoop

entry.title: Global Experts Establish New Name for PCOS to Reflect Multisystem Disease
entry.summary: Global Experts Establish New Name for PCOS to Reflect Multisystem Disease

entry.title: Oatmeal vs. Avocado Toast: Which Is Better for Lowering Cholesterol?
entry.summary: Nutrition experts share the scoop behind two popular breakfast staples and just how they stack up when it comes to lowering cholesterol.

entry.title: Why are some people mosquito magnets? Clues are emerging
entry.summary: Ever felt like mosquitoes bite you while ignoring everyone else? Scientists are now making progress in deciphering the complex chemical cocktail that makes particular people more enticing to these disease-spreading bloodsuckers.

entry.title: Personalized vaccine shows promise against aggressive brain cancer
entry.summary: A WashU Medicine-led clinical trial has found that a personalized vaccine to treat glioblastoma appears to increase recurrence-free survival in a subset of patients after surgery.

entry.title: She's trying to outrun pancreatic cancer. Breakthrough treatments give her hope
entry.summary: Pancreatic cancer is notoriously lethal. But new treatments mean that may be changing, and people with the disease now have more reason to hope than ever before.

entry.title: Engaging with the arts can slow biological aging, study shows
entry.summary: Eat well and exercise. That's the classic advice for living a long, healthy life. A new study suggests another habit to add to the list: Let your creative juices flow.

entry.title: Study finds engaging with the arts can slow biological aging
entry.summary: A new study finds that enjoyment of arts and culture slows down biological aging.

entry.title: They were on the trip of a lifetime. Now they’re quarantined as health officials work to contain a hantavirus outbreak
entry.summary: They boarded a cruise ship in Argentina last month for a once-in-a-lifetime expedition across the Atlantic Ocean to see unique wildlife and remote islands. Six weeks later, most of the American passengers who were aboard the MV Hondius are now in a small and …

entry.title: New Therapy Rewires the Brain To Restore Joy in Depression Patients
entry.summary: Scientists are rethinking how depression should be treated by focusing less on reducing negative feelings and more on rebuilding the brain’s capacity for positive emotion.

entry.title: New Daily Steps Goal Shows You Don't Need 10,000 to Keep The Weight Off
entry.summary: Hitting a weight-loss target is something to celebrate.

entry.title: The ‘Guardian’ Removed a Story About Hantavirus and Anxiety
entry.summary: The morning of May 12, the paper took down the article, with the note that the article ‘was removed after the Guardian was notified of a fundamental misunderstanding of remarks from Javier Padilla Bernáldez.’

entry.title: Common Sweetener May Harm Critical Brain Barrier, Risking Stroke
entry.summary: Found in everything from protein bars to energy drinks, erythritol has long been considered a safe alternative to sugar.

entry.title: Scientists learn how mosquitoes are able to track and bite humans
entry.summary: Researchers found that infrared radiation from a source at roughly the temperature of human skin doubles mosquitoes’ host-seeking behavior.

entry.title: Respiratory virus detected in Central Texas city amid hantavirus concerns
entry.summary: A man contracted measles after going overseas and later visiting an Austin medical center, per Texas health officials. The case comes amid hantavirus concerns.

entry.title: Caffeine may influence the way the brain responds to touch
entry.summary: Many people begin each day with a steaming cup of joe to shake off the morning fog and jump-start their brain. Whether it's a shot of espresso or a frothy latte, that caffeine hit is famous for boosting alertness. However, new research published in the journa…

entry.title: Hantavirus NC | Person in North Carolina from hantavirus cruise ship among those quarantined in Nebraska
entry.summary: A North Carolina resident is among the Americans flown to Nebraska for monitoring after being on board the cruise ship at the center of a deadly hantavirus outbreak.

entry.title: Hantavirus: 12 Radboud hospital workers to quarantine due to slack safety measures
entry.summary: A dozen workers at the university medical center in Nijmegen will enter a precautionary quarantine after possible exposure to hantavirus. The 12 employees at the Radboudumc hospital were involved in the case of a patient who tested positive for the viral infe…

entry.title: First Real-Time Brain-Controlled Hearing Device
entry.summary: Researchers demonstrate a real-time brain-controlled hearing device that selectively enhances voices based on a listener's neural activity.

entry.title: Why hantavirus is not like COVID, according to infectious disease experts
entry.summary: Infectious disease experts have sought to reassure people that the hantavirus cruise ship outbreak poses very low risks to the wider public.

entry.title: Norovirus-hit cruise docks at Port Canaveral after 115 fall ill on nightmare voyage
entry.summary: A Princess Cruises ship linked to a norovirus outbreak that sickened more than 100 passengers and crew members arrived at Port Canaveral.

entry.title: A New Hantavirus Vaccine Is in the Works
entry.summary: Since 2023, Moderna and Korea University have been developing a new mRNA vaccine for hantavirus. The work has been promising so far, but a finished product isn't likely coming any time soon.

entry.title: Moderna Stock Loses Steam. Hantavirus Trade Fizzles As Officials Dismiss Pandemic Risk.
entry.summary: Inovio Pharmaceuticals and Novavax stocks pare back earlier gains as the rally loses enthusiasm.

entry.title: The Next Frontier for Hantavirus: Finding Vaccines and Treatments
entry.summary: Some promising candidates are sitting on the shelf, awaiting funds before human tests

entry.title: Hantavirus Cruise Ship: American Patients Arrive—U.S. Downplays Risk( Live)
entry.summary: One of the American evacuees tested positive while another is showing mild symptoms, the HHS said.

entry.title: Exclusive | Ozempic and Mounjaro masked our cancer symptoms — even doctors were unconcerned
entry.summary: Some GLP-1 side effects overlap with symptomatic colorectal cancer, as two women who spoke to The Post learned the hard way. But a doctor reveals which GI issues are always a red flag.

entry.title: NY Sen. Schumer says hantavirus outbreak underscores dangers of CDC staffing cuts
entry.summary: The lawmaker says the White House should restore funding and rejoin the World Health Organization.

entry.title: Three enjoyable ways to slow your brain's ageing
entry.summary: Our brains thrive on a challenge, but it doesn't all have to be hard work to see the health benefits. Here are three simple and fun ways to protect your brain as you age.
